title: Geonoma occidentalis
taxonomy: Geonoma occidentalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae. This palm species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Geonoma occidentalis belongs to the genus Geonoma, which comprises about 75 species of palms. The Arecaceae family includes over 2,600 species of palms, making it one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Geonoma occidentalis is a relatively understudied species, but its classification as a member of the Arecaceae family provides a general understanding of its botanical identity.
